LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS SERVICE INC., DBA BLUE, SYNG KARROBI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES ON PLAINTIFF VIVEK VASUDEVA CLAIMS. MATTER ARGUED. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR ON THE REPRESENTATION THAT THE CLAIM WILL BE DISMISSED HEARING ON MOTION IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 22, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, FOR PURPOSES OF STATING THE RULING ONLY. NO FURTHER ARGUMENTS WILL BE ALLOWED AT THE HEARING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (302/HK)
ISSUING COMMISSION TO TAKE DEPOSITION OUT OF STATE UNDER CCP 2026 (CLARENCE CHOU, M.D.) FILED BY DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS SERVICE INC., DBA BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE AND SYNG KARROBI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THEIR ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS: A) GRANTED AS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGING CLAIMS FOR WRONGFUL DISCHARGE AND VIOLATIONS OF FEHA BASED ON HARASSMENT AND FAILURE TO PREVENT HARASSMENT (ISSUES 8 THROUGH 10 AND 12) AND THE ENTIRETY OF THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATIONS OF THE LABOR CODE (ISSUE 25); B) TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT (ISSUES 13-14) BECAUSE COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF VIVEK VASUDEVA STATED AT THE HEARING THAT THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION WOULD BE DISMISSED; AND C) DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION AND PORTIONS OF CAUSES OF ACTION (ISSUES 1-7 AND 15-24). THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES ON THE DISCRIMINATION ALLEGATIONS WHETHER: A) THE HR INVESTIGATION WHICH SERVED AS THE BASIS FOR THE DECISION TO TERMINATE MR. VASUDEVA'S EMPLOYMENT WAS INFLUENCED BY MS. KARROBI'S DISCRIMINATORY ANIMUS TOWARDS SOUTH ASIAN INDIANS; AND B) THE REASONS GIVEN FOR TERMINATING MR. VASUDEVA'S EMPLOYMENT WERE PRETEXTUAL. AS TO THE 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DEFAMATION AND IIED, THE SAME EVIDENCE CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MS. KARROBI'S STATEMENTS WERE MADE WITH MALICE FOR PURPOSES OF THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE. THERE IS ALSO SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE IN THE RECORD ON THIS MOTION TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO ALL ELEMENTS OF THOSE TWO CLAIMS. BLUE SHIELD'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTION 51 IS OVERRULED. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO BOTH THE DEFAMATION AND INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIMS ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE SAME EVIDENCE THAT SUPPORTS THE TRIABLE ISSUE OF NATIONAL ORIGIN DISCRIMINATION SUPPORTS A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO MALICE FOR PURPOSES OF THE PRIVILEGE. THE PARTIES SHALL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER AND CLARIFY ANY AMBIGUITY AS SET FORTH IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

Off Calendar Apr-10-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-13-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues On Plaintiff Vivek Vasudeva Claims
Off Calendar Mar-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Louis A. Highman, of Highman & Highman, Tel. 415.982.5563, for plaintiffs;
Stephanie Roeser, of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP, Tel. 415.291.7543, for defendants Blue Shield & Syng Karrobi;
Sharon Beth Bauman, of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP, Tel. 415.291.7425, for defendants Blue Shield & Syng Karrobi.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Defendant California Physicians' Service dba Blue Shield of California (Blue Shield) and Syng Karrobi's motion for summary judgment is denied and their alternative motion for summary adjudication is: a) granted as to the portions of the first and second causes of action alleging claims for wrongful discharge and violations of FEHA based on harassment and failure to prevent harassment (issues 8 through 10 and 12) and the entirety of the sixth cause of action for violations of the Labor Code (issue 25); b) taken off calendar as to the third cause of action for breach of contract (issues 13-14) because counsel for plaintiff Vivek Vasudeva stated at the hearing that the third cause of action would be dismissed; and c) denied as to all other causes of action and portions of causes of action (issues 1-7 and 15-24). Per Lilienthal & Fowler v. Superior Court (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1848, 1854, the harassment and failure to prevent harassment allegations in the first and second causes of action are treated as distinct claims amenable for resolution on this motion separate and distinct from the remaining allegations in the first and second causes of action. As to the harassment claims, Blue Shield has shown that the conduct complained of -- Courtney McCaslin's inquiries as to Mr. Vasudeva's sexual orientation and HR's response when Mr. Vasudeva stated he was gay -- was not sufficiently severe or pervasive to constitute actionable harassment and Mr. Vasudeva failed to present evidence creating a triable issue as to that issue. There are triable issues on the discrimination allegations whether: a) the HR investigation which served as the basis for the decision to terminate Mr. Vasudeva's employment was influenced by Ms. Karrobi's discriminatory animus towards South Asian Indians; and b) the reasons given for terminating Mr. Vasudeva's employment were pretextual. There is admissible evidence showing that Ms. Karrobi made comments that South Asian Indians do not make good leaders and are controlling. (Response to UMF 62hh.) There is also evidence Ms. Karrobi applied for Mr. Vasudeva's position at Blue Shield as an internal candidate but Mr. Vasudeva received the job as an external candidate instead. (Response to UMF 71f.) She thereafter reported to HR that Mr. Vasudeva sexually harassed Courtney McCaslin. (UMF 64.) This report of sexual harassment on September 23, 2014 sparked a hasty investigation ultimately leading to Mr. Vasudeva's termination for the unrelated reason of "conduct unbecoming of a leader" on October 3, 2014. (UMF 153 and 154.) Per Reid v. Google, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 512, all of this evidence is sufficient to create triable issues re animus and pretext. As to the fourth and fifth causes of action for defamation and IIED, the same evidence creates a triable issue whether Ms. Karrobi's statements were made with malice for purposes of the common interest privilege. There is also sufficient evidence in the record on this motion to create a triable issue as to all elements of those two claims. Blue Shield's evidentiary objection 51 is overruled. The Court denies summary adjudication as to both the defamation and intentional infliction of emotional distress claims on the grounds that the same evidence that supports the triable issue of national origin discrimination supports a triable issue as to malice for purposes of the privilege. The parties shall prepare a proposed order and clarify any ambiguity as set forth in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues On Plaintiff Vivek Vasudeva Claims
Off Calendar Mar-02-2017 Continued To Mar-22-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Louis Highman, of Highman & Highman, Tel. 415.982.5563, for plaintiffs Vivek Vasudeva;
Sharon Bauman, of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP, Tel. 415.291.7425, for defendants Blue Shield & Syng Karrobi;
Stephanie Roeser, of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP, Tel. 415.291.7543, for defendants Blue Shield & Syng Karrobi.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the 6th cause of action. The 3rd cause of action is taken off calendar on the representation that the claim will be dismissed. The DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS SERVICE INC., DBA BLUE, SYNG KARROBI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues On Plaintiff Vivek Vasudeva Claims is continued to March 22, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, for purposes of stating the ruling only. No further arguments will be allowed at the hearing. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
